calendar.matters:
Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement And General Mutual Release
Off Calendar Mar-02-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Barulich for (Petitioner) Steven Laub. Adrian Sawyer & Colin Kelley for and with Isabelle Laub.

Mr. Barulich stated that they filed a reply, that it sets forth statutory authorities, that it will address some of the court's issues and asked if the court had reviewed it. The Court responded that the examiner was not available and that it has not been reviewed. Mr. Sawyer stated that he does not think the reply changes anything. Mr. Barulich stated that they have a trial coming up on 3/12/18 and that he will respond to the notes here today; that the verification of the petition and reply are now filed, explained the calculation of the allocation to the beneficiaries, that there is no indemnity, cited 2772, that they have standard language in the settlement agreement, that the scope of the settlement addresses all claims, that it is effective and thorough and that there is no reference to the trust in the settlement agreement. The Court stated that its concern is how it can bind Mrs. Laub to an agreement that she is not a part of. Mr. Barulich stated that they have reached a good faith agreement and that if anyone has an issue she can defend it. The Court asked how it can approve a settlement agreement stating a claimant can pursue Mrs. Laub for an unpaid balance, that it cannot dictate the right of the parties. Mr. Barulich stated that he is not asking the court to rule on an amount, that the estate has struggled, referenced findings the court made in 2017, that 19402 applies, that the claim came in 2.5 years after Mr. Laub's death, that the estate has no money, that all of the beneficiaries except Mrs. Laub agree to settle and that she can settle or defend separately. Mr. Sawyer stated that over $653,000.00 in distributions were made to the children without a court order, that the trustee created a situation where the trust has no money and now wants to rely on 19402 and that the trustee should have held on to a reserve. Mr. Kelley stated that Mrs. Laub was the initial beneficiary and that the children were remainder beneficiaries, that if the funds were in the trust today there would not be an issue and that the trustee has an obligation to request that the residual beneficiaries return the $160,000.00 to settle the claim. The Court questioned the settlement providing for the claimant to be paid $50,000.00 by the children and $110,000.00 by Mrs. Laub. Mr. Barulich stated that they applied a percentage of the distributions, that Mrs. Laub can negotiate with the plaintiff , that Steve Laub did not distribute with knowledge of the claim and gave timelines of the claim being submitted. Mr. Kelley stated that the trustee had the power under 19402 to ask for the funds to be returned by the residual beneficiaries. Mr. Barulich stated that Mr. Kelley is misreading the statute and explained his position. Mr. Kelley stated that the trustee is skirting the rules, that if no distributions had been made there would be money in the trust to deal with this and that the claim was a matter of public record. The Court stated that both sides have argued extensively and that it will take the Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement and General Mutual Release under submission. Mr. Barulich reminded the court of the pending trial date and the court stated that it will have an order out in the next day or two.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Jung. CSR: Dawn Stark, CSR No. 7847, 415-362-4346.

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Oct-06-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. PES-16-299466, The Estate of Arnold Laub.

Appearances: Paul Barulich, Esq. for Steven A. Laub - Trustee/Petitioner.

Mr. Barulich stated that notice was given regarding today's appearance. Mr. Barulich presented argument in support of the petitions for instruction. At the conclusion of argument, the Court allowed supplemental briefing within 10 days. Any supplemental briefing is to be provided to the Court and opposing counsel. The matter will be submitted upon receipt of the supplemental briefing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Jul-12-2017 Continued To Aug-24-17 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Continued To Jul-12-17 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-14-297906 The Laub Revocable Trust and PES-16-299466 The Estate of Arnold Laub.

Appearances: Paul Barulich for Steven Laub.

Mr. Laub stated that the Plaintiff in the civil matter is not entitled to notice, that his Client is going to be the representative of the estate and that no service was made on any of the representatives of the estate in the civil matter. Mr. Laub also stated that probate was established merely to reject the creditor's claim in the probate case, that the real purpose of this is so that the complainant can not sit on it for two years, that there is $10,000.00 left in the estate and that there is another defendant (Decedent's law firm) in the case. In addition, Mr. Laub stated that there have been three Orders to Show Cause, that the probate case and the trust have a duty right to defend, that the creditor's claim affects service, that there is no duty with respect to the law firm and that there is an order that has already distributed. The Court stated that if it finds that they do not have to defend, that it will not rule on the merits. The Petitions for Instructions in both matters are continued to July 12, 2017 at 2:30 p.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition By Isabelle V. Laub For Approval Of Settlement Agreement And For Instructions That Trustee Make Distributions Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Colin Kelley for Isabelle Laub (Petitioner/beneficiary); Randy Schmidt for Steven A. Laub (Beneficiary); Edwin Hatter (Successor Trustee)
The Court inquired about the status of the Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement. Mr. Kelley responded that the Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement was discrete, that the necessary amended consent forms had been submitted, and that the matter was ready to be deemed submitted. Mr. Schmidt agreed. The Court inquired if any interim distribution was appropriate. Mr. Kelley replied that if any distribution should be made now, under the terms of the settlement agreement it should go to his client (Isabelle Laub), with a balance of $700,000 to be retained. The Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement was taken under submission.
Regarding the Petition for Removal of Trustee, Mr. Kelley advised the Court that an agreement
has been reached for the resignation of Mr. Hatter as Successor Trustee, and for his replacement by a new successor trustee. The Petition for Removal of Successor Trustee was continued to September 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that if the Settlement Agreement is filed and in order, the hearing on the Petition for Removal of Successor Trustee may go off calendar.
Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not reported)

Petition By Isabelle V. Laub For Approval Of Settlement Agreement And For Instructions That Trustee Make Distributions Pursuant To Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Continued To Aug-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Colin Kelly, for and with petitioner Isabelle Laub (beneficiary); Randall Schmidt for Steven Laub (bebeficiary); Edwin Hatter (Trustee).
Mr. Hatter advised the Court that his attorney, Alan Silver, was ill and would not be present.

The Court stated that he had only recently received the settlement agreement between the parties, and needed additional time to review the Petition to Approve the Settlement Agreement and for Instructions. He suggested it would be appropriate to continue both petitions, and asked how long the hearing should be continued. Mr. Schmidt replied that there was a hearing on a companion case set for August 3, 2015, and recommended continuance of the Petition for Removal of Trustee be continued to that date. The Petition for Removal of trustee was continued to August 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Mr. Kelly stated that he would like the Court to move forward on the Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement and for Instructions. Mr. Schmidt stated that he believed the two petitions were discrete and could be heard separately, but he was still in the process of obtaining revised consents from additional beneficiaries. The Court set July 20, 2015 as the deadline for submission of the consents and any other supplemental papers. If everything outstanding has been filed with the Court by that date the matter will be under submission as of that date; if not, the hearing on the Petition for Approval of the Settlement Agreement and for Instructions is continued to August 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy K. Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Apr-20-2015 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust.
Off Calendar Jan-14-2015 Continued To Apr-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jamay Lee appeared by telephone for Steven Laub. Collin Kelley for and with Isabelle Laub (Objector/Beneficiary).

Ms. Lee stated that mediation is definitely a possibility and that she believes her Client would be open to mediation. Mr. Kelley stated that the Trustee noted that the value of the estate is much lower than they had believed, that the petition is now moot and that it should go away. Ms. Lee argued that there is nothing before the Court to show that the value is below $3,000,000.00. Mr. Kelley requested submitting an accounting to the Court and stated that there is some outstanding discovery. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than April 6, 2015. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by April 3, 2015. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust was continued to and the Parties are to return on April 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Clerk provided Mr. Kelley with a copy of the mediation order and will fax a copy to Ms. Lee.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy C. Clark. (Not Reported)
